content:
## Index Coverage & Staleness Detection — Implementation Complete

### What
Added `index_coverage`, `index_stale`, and 5 supporting fields to `GET /api/dashboard/status`. New `POST /api/dashboard/status/refresh-coverage` endpoint for on-demand recomputation. Three-layer comparison: disk files vs Redb SCOPE_INDEX vs HNSW_TO_UUID.

### Architecture
- `IndexCoverageCache` uses `std::sync::Mutex` (not `tokio::sync::Mutex`) with brief lock holds — avoids complex handler futures with dual axum 0.7/0.8. Async `refresh()` does spawn_blocking for disk I/O.
- Background loop in main.rs refreshes coverage every `coverage_ttl_secs` (default 60s). TTL is captured once at startup — hot-reload changes require restart despite `dashboard` being in HOT_RELOADABLE_SECTIONS.
- `CoverageResponse` typed struct shared between status and refresh endpoints (eliminates untyped serde_json::Value divergence).

### Key Design Decisions
- Coverage = hnsw_count / disk_count (not store_count). Clamped to 1.0 when orphaned entries exist.
- `index_stale` uses strict less-than (`<`), not `<=` — coverage exactly at threshold is NOT stale.
- `count_json_files` is flat (non-recursive) matching current knowledge file layout.
- PostgresStore fallback: hnsw_count = store_count (pgvector manages its own index).

### Review Findings (deferred for follow-up)
- Nest 7 flat fields under `index_coverage` object (API breaking change)
- Add `index_scope_id` field (multi-scope awareness)
- Add remediation hints when stale
- Add orphan detection convenience field
- Rate-limit refresh endpoint
